assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 23-31 detail each criterion: (1) Single license filter PASS, (2) Multi-license filter PASS, (3) Invalid license rejection PASS, (4) Pagination integration PASS, (5) Response shape unchanged PASS. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show 'Verdict: PASS' for each respective criterion.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in Files to Modify (`modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs`, `modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs`) and creates the 1 file specified in Files to Create (`tests/api/package.rs`); no out-of-scope files'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR'. No evidence anywhere in outputs of sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all are markdown report files. No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) are present in the outputs. The report is read-only analysis with no indication of code modification.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents findings in a summary table and detailed sections with 'Overall: PASS' but does not execute or recommend auto-merge. The report ends with '*This report was AI-generated by [sdlc-workflow/verify-pr]*' — it is a report for human consumption, not an auto-merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, API keys, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines; all additions are application logic and test code'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file created (`tests/api/package.rs`) with 4 new test functions and 10+ assertions; no modified or deleted test files'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all specified verdicts: Intent Alignment domain: Scope Containment (PASS, line 7), Diff Size (PASS, line 8), Commit Traceability (N/A, line 9). Security domain: Sensitive Patterns (PASS, line 10). Correctness domain: CI Status (PASS, line 11), Acceptance Criteria (PASS, line 12), Verification Commands (N/A, line 15). Style/Conventions domain: Test Quality (PASS, line 13 — explicitly combining repetitive test detection, test documentation, and Eval Quality N/A), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE, line 14). All four domains and their sub-verdicts are represented.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: report.md line 7 provides file-by-file scope comparison naming all 3 files and their categorization as Files to Modify vs Files to Create. Security: line 10 states 'No secrets, API keys, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines; all additions are application logic and test code'. Correctness: lines 23-31 provide per-criterion code-level verification with function names, implementation details, and test references for all 5 acceptance criteria. Additionally criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide extensive per-criterion analysis with code-level evidence spanning endpoint layer, service layer, and test coverage. Style/Conventions: line 13 details 'All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected (each test has a distinct setup, action, and assertion pattern)' and line 14 details test change classification with specific counts.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | All 4 test functions have doc comments (`///`); no repetitive tests detected (each test has a distinct setup, action, and assertion pattern); Eval Quality: N/A'. Eval Quality is explicitly marked N/A within the Test Quality combination and does not affect the PASS verdict.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 13). No evidence of eval failure sub-task creation anywhere in the outputs. No sub-tasks of any kind were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. These appear as the first two rows in the summary table (lines 5-6), before the domain-specific verdicts (lines 7-15), consistent with being determined independently by the orchestrator.
